title: Falling Fertility Is a Bipartisan Tragedy | Institute for Family Studies
gist: Grant J. Bailey, writing in Commonplace, argues America's fertility decline is splitting along partisan lines — Trump-voting counties, red states, and blue-to-red migrant parents all show higher fertility — and warns that conservatives celebrating this as a demographic win are missing that the overall collapse in family formation is a bipartisan tragedy, not a partisan victory.

insights.claim: Counties that voted for Donald Trump in the 2024 election had higher fertility rates than counties that voted for Kamala Harris, and Republican-leaning states have higher overall fertility rates than Democratic-leaning states.
insights.speaker: Grant J. Bailey
insights.so_what: Documents a widening political fertility divide with long-run implications for electoral demographics and regional population/labor-force growth.
insights.quote: counties that voted for Donald Trump in the 2024 election had higher fertility rates than ones that voted for Kamala Harris

insights.claim: Parents are more likely to relocate from Democratic-leaning ('blue') states to Republican-leaning ('red') states, reinforcing the geographic concentration of higher fertility in red states.
insights.speaker: Grant J. Bailey
insights.so_what: A migration-driven feedback loop that could accelerate regional demographic and political realignment, relevant to state-level population and tax-base forecasts.
insights.quote: parents are more likely to move from blue states to red ones

insights.claim: Bailey warns that some right-leaning commentators are treating the emerging fertility gap as a political victory lap toward a more conservative future electorate, but frames the overall fertility collapse as a bipartisan tragedy rather than a partisan win, since the gap is projected to widen further as the next generation takes shape.
insights.speaker: Grant J. Bailey
insights.so_what: Pushes back on framing declining fertility as good news for either political side — a corrective for anyone modeling pronatalist policy or political-demographic trends as a partisan story.
insights.locator: Some right-leaning commentators are running victory laps
